module_description: Monitors SNMP-enabled devices. Define servers once, then attach any number of OID checks to each server. Supports SNMP v1, v2c and v3 (authPriv).

hints.threads: Maximum number of OID checks to run in parallel.
hints.mib_dirs: Comma-separated list of additional directories containing compiled Python MIB modules (output of mibdump.py / pysmi). Used to resolve OID names and types during discovery. The application data folder (snmp_mibs/compiled/) is always searched automatically — drop raw ASN.1 .mib files into snmp_mibs/raw/ for automatic compilation (requires pysmi). Leave empty to use only the built-in RFC/IETF MIBs.
hints.label: Display name shown in the UI. Falls back to the item key if empty.
hints.host: IP address or hostname of the SNMP device.
hints.community: Community string for SNMP v1/v2c authentication.
hints.timeout: Seconds to wait for each SNMP response.
hints.retries: Number of retries on timeout before declaring failure.
hints.snmpv3_priv_key: SNMP v3 privacy (encryption) passphrase. Leave empty for authNoPriv.
hints.snmpv3_auth_protocol: Hash algorithm for SNMP v3 authentication.
hints.snmpv3_priv_protocol: Encryption algorithm for SNMP v3 privacy.
hints.server: Key of the server entry (from the Servers section) to use for this check. Leave empty to use the first enabled server.
hints.oid: OID to query in dotted-decimal notation (e.g. 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.1.0 for sysDescr). Use Discover to browse available OIDs.
hints.operator: Comparison operator. Use 'any' to check reachability only.
hints.value: Expected value for the comparison. Numeric operators (gt/lt/gte/lte) cast the OID value to float.
hints.alert: Consecutive failures before triggering an alert.

rename_item_prompt: Enter new entry name or identifier:

ui.discover_error: Could not connect to any configured server.
ui.mib_delete_selected_confirm: Delete {n} selected file(s)? This cannot be undone.
ui.mib_no_raw: No .mib files yet — upload one to get started
ui.mib_cancel_confirm: Cancel the ongoing compilation and close?
ui.mib_details_no_symbols: No symbols loaded — pysnmp unavailable or module failed to load
